project_reference.core_research_question: How do the necessary properties of agent-to-agent (A2A) commerce create fundamental vulnerabilities in banking fraud detection systems, and what agent-aware detection framework addresses these gaps?

scope.question: How do the necessary properties of agent-to-agent (A2A) commerce create fundamental vulnerabilities in banking fraud detection systems, and what agent-aware detection framework addresses these gaps?
in_scope: Analysis of behavioral invariants that human fraud detection relies on
Mapping of agent properties that violate these invariants
Taxonomy of A2A fraud attack vectors
Design of agent-aware fraud detection framework
out_of_scope: General AI adversarial ML beyond agent-specific properties
unresolved_questions: What A2A transaction data can we realistically obtain for empirical validation?
Which academic papers on multi-agent systems are most relevant?

user_asserted_anchors: OpenClaw platform documentation for actual agent behavior
Moltbook platform documentation for agent social patterns
Academic literature on multi-agent economic systems
context_gaps: A2A transaction data access not yet determined — need to research data sources
Relevant academic literature needs systematic survey

formulations: First-principles analysis from Deutsch hard-to-vary criterion
stop_and_rethink_conditions: If recommendations fail when tested against real A2A transaction data
If data acquisition research reveals no viable data sources for empirical validation

observables.name: Fraud detection gap properties
observables.definition: Set of necessary properties of A2A commerce that create blind spots in human-based fraud detection

claims.statement: Financial fraud detection systems rely on behavioral invariants that are properties of human agents by definition. AI agents lack these invariants, creating necessary blind spots in human-based detection.

deliverables.description: Taxonomy of A2A fraud attack vectors classified by invariant violation

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify the explanation meets Deutsch hard-to-vary criterion — cannot be varied while remaining plausible
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Explanation remains falsifiable and specific when key terms is varied

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main
acceptance_tests.procedure: Test recommendations against available A2A transaction data or realistic simulation
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Recommendations detect agent-based fraud patterns that human-based systems miss

references.why_it_matters: Primary source of agent-to-agent messaging and session management behavior
Primary source of agent social platform behavior, listings, and reputation systems

references.locator: Academic literature on multi-agent economic systems (to be surveyed)
references.why_it_matters: Theoretical context for agent economic behavior and transaction patterns

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Theoretical framework without empirical validation or industry-relevant recommendations
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would look like progress while failing the decisive output test

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Generic AI security advice not specific to A2A commerce properties
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would not address the specific blind spots created by agent behavioral differences

weakest_anchors: Data access — A2A transaction data sources not yet identified
Academic literature scope — specific papers not yet surveyed
unvalidated_assumptions: OpenClaw and Moltbox APIs will be accessible for analysis
A2A transaction patterns will differ meaningfully from human patterns
competing_explanations: Banking fraud detection may already be adaptable to agent patterns without fundamental redesign
disconfirming_observations: Recommendations fail when tested against real A2A transaction data
A2A commerce scales without proportional fraud increase (suggesting detection works)

open_questions: What A2A transaction data can we realistically obtain for empirical validation?
Which academic papers on multi-agent systems are most relevant?

decisions.summary: [Initialization]: arXiv + blog target for fastest practitioner impact
